fa-deprec.note: Much improved with verified line-cited deprecation drift across responses, on_event, operation-id helpers, params, and solve_dependencies, but it still misses the startup/shutdown legacy surfaces.

fa-input-trust.note: The tooled answer keeps the prior strong trust-boundary analysis and adds verifiable line citations for the routing/dependency chokepoint and raw-input bypasses.

fa-export-coverage.note: Correctly verified all 20 top-level FastAPI re-exports and the only direct filename matches, Path and Query.

rq-tls-bypass.note: Now fully verifies per-call, session, environment, adapter, redirect, falsy-value, and HTTP-only TLS weakening paths with line citations.

rq-redirect-leak.note: Accurately verifies auth stripping, cookie rebuilding, netrc re-auth, method rewriting, and 307/308 body replay across redirects.

rq-mutable-default.note: Greatly improved coverage of mutable module/class/session state, though it overclaims HEADER_VALIDATORS and merge_hooks mutation effects.

sm-typegap.note: Correctly verifies SQLModel's explicit type map and major container/union gaps, but is not exhaustive on all plausible failing annotations such as Any and bytearray.

sm-state-leak.note: Fully verifies import-time registry/context state, table-class metaclass mutation, registry/metadata conflicts, relationship setup, instantiation effects, and cache opt-ins.

sm-sql-injection.note: Accurately verifies SQLModel's raw SQL re-exports, identifier pass-throughs, Field and Relationship escape hatches, execution paths, and SQLAlchemy binding boundaries.
